description:
A media processing background service writes its transcoding queue length to '/var/log/transcoder/status.log'. You must configure OpManager to monitor this proprietary application metric.

First, create a CLI (Linux/SSH) credential named exactly 'Local-Script-Exec' with the username 'ga' and password 'password123'. 

Second, create a Script Template named exactly 'Video-Transcoder-Queue' with Output Type 'Numeric' that executes the script: 'bash /opt/custom_monitors/check_transcode_queue.sh'. 

Finally, associate this script monitor to the localhost device (127.0.0.1) using the new credential, and configure a Critical threshold condition to alert if the queue length is > 500.

Log in to OpManager at http://localhost:8060 using credentials admin / Admin@123.
